_comment: Denarixx Vision Glasses — First Physical Prototype Hardware Profile
_status: bring-up — not mass-production, not final component selection

profile.name: Denarixx Vision Prototype P0
profile.purpose: First bring-up validation of standalone wearable vision pipeline

compute.os: Linux (Yocto-based minimal rootfs or Raspberry Pi OS Lite 64-bit)

cpu.example_soc: Rockchip RK3588S or Raspberry Pi CM4 (vendor TBD)
cpu._note: No final SoC selected. Target: 4-core ARM64, ≥1.5 GHz, TDP ≤ 3W at sustained load

npu._note: NPU optional for P0 bring-up; CPU inference fallback required

cameras._note: Front camera is primary safety sensor. All cameras are optional for P0; front alone is minimum viable.

microphone.purpose: Wake word detection and voice commands
microphone._note: Dual-mic beamforming preferred but single-mic acceptable for P0

audio_output._note: Bone-conduction is the primary output for non-disruptive ambient-aware audio. Wired 3.5mm jack fallback.

haptic.type: ERM (Eccentric Rotating Mass) or LRA (Linear Resonant Actuator)
haptic.interface: PWM or I2C haptic driver IC (e.g. DRV2605L or equivalent)
haptic._note: LRA preferred for precise pattern control. PWM fallback for P0.

imu._note: Separate magnetometer recommended for heading accuracy

gnss._note: GNSS optional for indoor P0 testing; required for outdoor navigation

lte_esim._note: LTE/eSIM optional M.2 slot — not populated in P0 bring-up

monitoring.ic: I2C fuel gauge (e.g. MAX17048 or BQ27220 equivalent)

thermal.sensor: I2C temperature sensor on compute module and near battery

hal._note: Guardian and navigation engines must not know which adapter is active

inference.capability_honesty: Every detection class declares: model-supported | heuristic | experimental | unavailable
inference.simulation_mode: development-only — never active during real-user operation
